LanguageData.Greenway_Precept_MutantCitizenship.label: mutant citizenship
LanguageData.Greenway_Precept_MutantCitizenship.descr: Mutants are accepted as full members of the community. Their altered bodies are no barrier to belonging so long as they stand with the colony.
LanguageData.Greenway_Precept_SecondClassMutants.desc: Mutants may remain inside society, but only in a lesser caste. They are useful when kept subordinate and put to work under firm supervision.
LanguageData.Greenway_Precept_MutantSlaves.label: mutant slaves
LanguageData.Greenway_Precept_MutantSlaves.descriptio: Mutants are valued primarily as a slave caste. They are strong, dangerous, and best kept in chains where their labor can be exploited safely.
LanguageData.Greenway_Precept_MutantPurge.label: mutant purge
LanguageData.Greenway_Precept_MutantPurge.description: Mutants are treated as a stain on civilization. Their presence in the colony is dangerous, degrading, and should be stamped out wherever possible.
LanguageData.Greenway_Precept_MutantMasters.label: mutant masters
LanguageData.Greenway_Precept_MutantMasters.descripti: Mutants are seen as the natural overseers of the wastes. Their strength grants them the right to rule, especially over unmutated laborers kept beneath them.
LanguageData.Greenway_Precept_Utobitha.label: Utobitha
LanguageData.Greenway_Precept_Utobitha.description: A true mutant homeland should be built by mutants and for mutants. Baseline wastelanders are tolerated only as a problem, never as rightful members of the order being created.
